items.title: As the battle for AI supremacy intensifies, Trump White House reportedly contemplates a ban on Chinese models
items.description: Not only do Chinese models perform many of the same tasks for a fraction of the cost of U.S. versions, their prospective flotations may dilute the appetite for mega-IPOs like Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: Scared of the AI trade? Here are three investment themes Goldman Sachs is offering instead.
items.description: Semiconductors have fallen into a bear market, prompting some investors to look elsewhere

items.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
items.description: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

items.title: Domino’s shares jump as franchise store operators spend more on ingredients
items.description: The pizza chain’s stock jumped about 7% in premarket trading after revenue was around 2.5% higher than analysts’ estimates for the second quarter.

items.title: This may be the real reason USDA made a huge revision to its beef-export sales data
items.description: It’s ‘like driving one more nail into the proverbial coffin,’ says a rancher about the recent deterioration in U.S. agriculture data

items.title: World Cup champion Spain just won $50 million — and the IRS gets a cut
items.description: “It doesn’t make a difference who wins the game. The IRS will get a piece.”

items.title: Oil prices reverse lower after report of new Iran cease-fire proposal
items.description: Oil prices reversed lower after a report of a 10-day ceasefire proposal came after Iran’s foreign ministry said negotiations with the U.S. could be pursued.

items.title: I racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
items.description: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program.”

items.title: I will claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: Why it’s time to retire the Magnificent Seven as a stock-market talking point, say these strategists
items.description: Magnificent Seven as a group is underperforming the broader market this year, but strategists at Citi argue it’s no longer a grouping that even makes sense to think about it.
